column1: # Cross-model tolerance overrides for Layer 4 (Issue #224, Decision 9).
# When the raw-array (`default` scenario) and bin-counter (`bin-data-model`
# scenario) data models disagree on a value, Layer 4 classifies the deviation
# against a tier ladder. Default ladder is:
#   T1  bitwise identical                (advisory)
#   T2  within +/-1%                     (advisory)
#   T3  within +/-4%                     (advisory)
#   T4  beyond +/-4%                     (blocking)
# Rows in this file override `t2_pct` and `t3_pct` for specific
# (scenario, column) pairs. Tuning is expected: bin-counter implementations
# quantize samples into bin boundaries, so percentile drift relative to raw
# is bounded by bin width at the relevant percentile. Wider tolerances on
# p99999 than on p50 are normal.
# Schema (tab-separated):
#   scenario   non-blank for per-scenario override, blank for global-by-column
#   column     non-blank for per-column override, blank for global-by-scenario
#   t2_pct     numeric percent for the T2 advisory ceiling
#   t3_pct     numeric percent for the T3 advisory ceiling (T4 is everything beyond)
#   notes      free-form documentation; required, not optional
# Resolution rules (most-specific wins):
#   1. (scenario, column) both non-blank   applies only to that pair
#   2. scenario blank, column non-blank    applies to that column across all scenarios
#   3. scenario non-blank, column blank    applies to all columns of that scenario
#   4. both blank                          syntax error, engine refuses to start
#   5. no matching row                     ladder defaults apply
# Today, the bin-counter implementation does not exist for the per-message-key
# and per-time-bucket surfaces; -mdm bin / -bdm bin silently fall back to raw.
# Layer 4 reports T1 on every paired cell as a wiring sanity check until the
# bin implementation lands. This file ships with no data rows for that reason.
# When bin lands, populate this file with the tolerance envelopes appropriate
# for each scenario/column pair.
# scenario
